title: erigeron philadelphicus subsp. provancheri
intro: Erigeron philadelphicus subsp. provancheri, also known as Philly fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of Erigeron philadelphicus subsp. provancheri typically involve infusions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of salves or poultices.
